name: Worldpay Global Payment Gateway API
what_it_does: Worldpay global payment gateway REST API for large enterprises to accept card payments, manage global acquiring relationships, process multi-currency transactions, and handle fraud management across 146 countries with direct acquiring network access. Enables AI agents to manage payment authorization and capture for enterprise transaction automation, handle global currency and payment method processing for international commerce automation, access fraud scoring and risk management for payment security automation, retrieve transaction reporting and settlement for financial reconciliation automation, manage recurring payment configuration for subscription billing automation, handle refund and chargeback management for dispute resolution automation, access payment tokenization for PCI-compliant storage automation, retrieve acquiring bank relationship management for enterprise payment routing automation, manage alternative payment method integration (PayPal, Alipay, iDEAL) for global payment method automation, and integrate Worldpay with SAP, Oracle, and enterprise ERP systems for end-to-end global enterprise payment management.
use_cases: Processing global enterprise card payments from payment orchestration agents
Managing multi-currency acquiring from international commerce agents
Handling chargeback disputes from payment risk management agents
Integrating alternative payment methods from global checkout agents
not_for: Developer-friendly startup payments (use Stripe for modern developer experience)
Marketplace payments (use Adyen or Stripe Connect for marketplace)
SMB payment processing (use Square or Authorize.net for small business)
best_when: A Fortune 500 retailer or enterprise wanting AI agents to automate global payment processing, multi-currency acquiring, fraud management, and enterprise ERP payment integration through Worldpay's direct acquiring network (FIS subsidiary).
avoid_when: ENTERPRISE IMPLEMENTATION AND INTEGRATION COMPLEXITY: Worldpay enterprise integration requires merchant services contract, dedicated integration team, and implementation engagement; automated self-service Worldpay integration is not the intended model; automated standalone integration without enterprise team creates gaps in acquiring configuration and routing. WORLDPAY FIS PRODUCT FRAGMENTATION: Worldpay has multiple product lines (Worldpay from FIS, Worldpay for Business, Worldpay Total) with different APIs and capabilities; automated integration must identify target Worldpay product line; automated cross-product API calls create endpoint not found errors. ACQUIRING BANK RELATIONSHIP REGIONAL VARIATION: Worldpay's direct acquiring relationships vary by region; automated global payment routing depends on region-specific acquiring configuration; automated global payment flow without region-specific configuration creates sub-optimal routing and higher decline rates in some markets.

auth.notes: Worldpay uses API key and OAuth for integrations. REST API with JSON. Cincinnati, Ohio HQ (London UK operations). Founded 1989. Acquired by FIS in 2019 for $43B. Products: Payment gateway, global acquiring, fraud management, alternative payment methods, recurring billing. SDKs: Java, .NET, PHP, Python. PCI DSS Level 1. Serves global enterprises in 146 countries. Competes with Adyen, CyberSource, and Checkout.com for enterprise payment gateway.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise contract required
pricing.paid_tiers: Custom enterprise pricing based on volume and market; interchange-plus or bundled rates negotiated per contract
pricing.notes: Cincinnati OH. FIS subsidiary. Custom enterprise pricing. Annual contract with negotiated rates.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; transaction processing throttled by acquirer; webhooks for payment, refund, and dispute events

known_agent_gotchas: PRODUCT LINE API FRAGMENTATION: Worldpay has separate APIs for different product lines (Online Payments, Worldpay for Business); automated integration must target correct API documentation for specific Worldpay product; automated cross-product API call creates 404 or authentication error

XML-BASED LEGACY API vs JSON REST API COEXISTENCE: Worldpay maintains both legacy XML API and newer JSON REST API; automated integration should target JSON REST API; automated XML API integration creates technical debt as Worldpay shifts to REST; automated migration path from XML to REST requires endpoint remapping

3DS2 FLOW MANDATORY FOR EU/UK MERCHANTS: European and UK Worldpay merchants must implement 3DS2 Strong Customer Authentication for card-not-present transactions; automated EU payment flow must implement complete 3DS2 protocol; automated non-3DS EU payment creates declined authorization for missing SCA compliance

MERCHANT CODE vs INSTALLATION ID FOR MULTI-MID: Worldpay uses Merchant Code and Installation ID to identify merchant accounts; multi-entity enterprises have multiple merchant codes; automated payment routing to correct merchant entity must maintain merchant code registry per legal entity

SETTLEMENT CURRENCY vs TRANSACTION CURRENCY RECONCILIATION: Worldpay processes multi-currency transactions but settles in merchant's configured settlement currency; automated reconciliation must account for DCC (dynamic currency conversion) and settlement currency conversion; automated reconciliation using transaction currency without exchange rate creates settlement amount discrepancy
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise payments. PCI-DSS Level 1. GDPR. API key/OAuth. US/EU/APAC. Payment and transaction data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise payments. PCI-DSS Level 1. GDPR. API key/OAuth. US/EU/APAC. Payment data.
